diagrams.title: NFA for Pattern 'aba'
diagrams.description: State diagram showing the 4-state NFA constructed from pattern 'aba' using the standard pattern-to-NFA construction. Each state represents how much of the pattern has been matched as suffix of processed input.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how NFA tracks pattern matching progress, showing that accepting state q₃ is reached exactly when 'aba' appears as suffix of processed text.

diagrams.title: DFA State Diagram for Pattern 'ab'
diagrams.description: Complete DFA with 3 states for pattern 'ab', showing transitions for both alphabet symbols a and b. This is the minimal DFA by Myhill-Nerode theorem.
elements: Transitions labeled with a,b from each state
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the deterministic nature - from any state on any symbol, exactly one transition. Shows how DFA processes text one character at a time.

diagrams.title: Text Search as Language Recognition
diagrams.description: Conceptual diagram showing the relationship: Input Text → [DFA/NFA] → Accept/Reject. If accepted, pattern was found as substring.
elements: Accept output when reaching accepting state
Match positions indicated by accepting state entries
diagrams.purpose: Reinforce the core automata-theoretic view: text search is language recognition - checking if text belongs to language Σ*PΣ*
